author: @Therapistinthewhitehouse
text: With the milage he’s doing he’s probably only saving around £110 a year on fuel but he’s getting fitter and saving wear and tear on his car. I as he said he only used his car twice in that time there comes a point where it’s not worth owning and the money saved on all the running costs can be used to get a taxi or join a car club like Zipcar. I did this a couple of years ago and I must have saved thousands in insurance premiums, fuel, depreciation, car loans, road tax, servicing, repairs, tyres, parking charges, permits and fines etc. In that time I’ve only needed a car once and a van 4 times to help people move home and the rest of the time I use the tube or busses. The Zip cars come with 60mile fuel allowance per trip and because I don’t need more don’t cost a penny more than the hourly hire charge which is under £10 for a car an around £12 for a van.

author: @nattsurfaren
text: Hey Robert, I was wondering if e-bike kits and how to install them would interest your viewers. I got my second e-bike kit over 10 years ago and I had difficulty installing it because I didn't know how to connect the wires I got batteries that had Anderson connectors and I didn't know how to make it work with my controller. So I got help from a guy that knew much more than me and he helped me with most things but we couldn't make the e-bike run that day and we didn't know what the problem was. Well, the controller needed a peddle sensor to make it work. So I got help with that from another guy. Then It worked nicely but the bike was wickedly overpowered with 72V. The front fork got bent because of the torque. So that day I learned that I needed torque arms mounted to alleviate the stress from the motor. I created my own torque arm and then I mounted it on my bike and then I could ride it and it was amazing.

author: @dav1dbone
text: Yes to the review, consise and relevant, had a few ebikes myself and worked with many lithium batteries, can say that the power meters are never as useful as one may think, it's just a representation of voltage, not amp hour capacity, albeit through usage the rider may gain familiarisation. My 1st bike did 50kms,the meter would read 5bar for little use, drop to 4 pretty quickly, stay there for 45kms,once it hit the 3rd bar it rapidly became obvious it was loosing juice. Always found bits would come loose and fall off, having cable ties in panniers are a must.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice add mate - thanks for taking the time to post

replies.author: @DaveJSmith
replies.text:
Yes, I can see that the meter is reading voltage since it drops when it is doing some work, such as a steep bit uphill, and eventually goes back up a bar when the going gets easier. However, over time it seems to give a reasonable indication of range.

Dave

text: That was a great review altho it left me with two questions. The first is did he enjoy riding the bike? Did it make him want to take the bike for rides? The second question is does a mode exist where he doesn't have to pedal at all?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: he is a greta guy and he told me he loved it and he has more or less abandoned his car altogether - if that helps - also it is illegal to ride an bike here in the UK under throttle only - so you must pedal - I think there is a more powerful US version that is throttle only but you would have to check the eskute site to be sure mate

replies.author: @DaveJSmith
replies.text:
Hello Brett

I absolutely love this bike. I did think that I would just use it for those times when I didn't have the time to walk to the shop instead of using the car. However, I find myself using this bike above anything else and I find myself looking for an excuse to go out on it ... hence the trip to Durham.

Dave

replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I think I mean your brother who lives near Durham.  I think I missed it when he said he rode there.  He mentioned how long it took him by car. But not by ebike.  I couldn't rewind the video as I had dirty hands I was listening doing something else.

replies.text:
@clangerbasher Durham is about 15 miles form my house and takes about an hour to ride there

Dave

replies.author: @clangerbasher
replies.text: @DaveJSmith Thank you.  It just nice to get more data re range and time etc.  I think the Polluno represents the best value point for these things.  You can get some nice bikes for about £1800.  But you are into 50cc or electric equivalent territory at that point.  And I would rather take that option even though it means insurance etc. I think the licensing and power bands for bikes below 15hp need to be looked at.  I think a 1kw limit would really energise the market for these bikes.

replies.author: @DaveJSmith
replies.text:
@clangerbasher For me it is about having a bit if a work out and not needing to use the car, I am less interested in the motor part than I am in the bicycle part. But it certainly is a fertile area of development and I don't doubt that there is some money to be made for someone who makes the right choices!!

Dave
